business.headline: Iran may impose transit fees on ships to pass through Strait of HormuzSince the start of the US-Israeli war on Iran, Tehran has disrupted maritime transit through the strait for vessels it says are linked to its war adversaries and their allies.

Gold down nearly Rs 7,000, silver drops Rs 20,000: Buy, sell or hold now?Gold and silver prices plunged sharply today, with gold losing around Rs 7,000 and silver dropping Rs 20,000. This fall has buyers and investors debating whether to invest now or wait for a recovery.

Choice Mutual Fund rolls out two new fund offerings. Details hereThe two new schemes, i.e., Choice Nifty 50 Index Fund and Choice Nifty Next 50 Index Fund, are designed to give investors exposure to India's equity market without the complexity of actively managed funds.

Sensex crashes over 2,200 points as West Asia war escalatesIndian stock markets witnessed a severe crash as the Sensex plunged over 2200 points and Nifty fell by 687 points, down nearly 3 per cent, due to escalating conflict in West Asia.

Government looks to US, Australia and Russia for fuel as LPG shortage deepensWith multiple energy hubs under threat, the risk to global supply chains has increased sharply. For countries like India, which depend heavily on imports, this means higher costs and tighter supply in the near term.
Sensex ends 2,500 points lower, Nifty below 23,100; HDFC down 5%The S&P BSE Sensex plummeted 2,496.89 points to close at 74,207.24, while the NSE Nifty50 tanked 775.65 points to end at 23,002.15.

How the Hormuz disruption could make South Mumbai flats more expensiveA ripple from far beyond India's shores is beginning to touch Mumbai's skyline. If you're planning to buy a South Mumbai flat, you may soon notice prices inching higher.

How Iran is making Wall Street bleed in retaliation to US strikesSince February 27, all three major US indices have declined. The Dow Jones Industrial Average has fallen about 2,752 points, or around 5.6%. The S&P 500 is down about 254 points, or 3.7%, while the Nasdaq Composite has dropped around 515 points, or 2.3%.

Is crypto the new market favourite amid ongoing West Asia conflict?As tensions in West Asia push oil prices higher and keep global markets on edge, investors are once again looking for safety. But this time, alongside gold and the dollar, crypto is gaining attention — is it becoming the new market favourite? Let's have a look.

HDFC Bank shares drop after chairman's exit. Should investors buy, sell or wait?HDFC Bank's fall has left investors at a crossroads. With strong fundamentals but lingering uncertainty, the big question now is simple: should you buy the dip, sell, or just wait?

Is global recession just weeks away amid Middle East conflict? Neelkanth Mishra, Chief Economist at Axis Bank, said that around 7% of the world's energy supply is currently affected due to the conflict, which is a major concern for global growth.

No material concerns at HDFC Bank, says RBI after chairman's exitIn a statement, the central bank said it has taken note of the recent developments at HDFC Bank and has approved the transition arrangement proposed by the bank for the position of part-time chairman.

Brent crude prices jump sharply to $118 as Middle East conflict escalatesBrent Crude has climbed to $114.15 per barrel, while US West Texas Intermediate (WTI) rose to $97.13. Back in India, crude oil prices on the Multi Commodity Exchange (MCX) also went up by more than1%, reaching Rs 9,099 per barrel.
Anil Ambani questioned by CBI in multi-crore State Bank loan 'fraud' caseAnil Ambani has been called in for questioning again tomorrow by the CBI. Today he was questioned for 7.30 hours.

No major issues at HDFC Bank, says Keki Mistry after chairman's sudden exitSpeaking on an investor call, Keki Mistry said there are no major issues at the bank and indicated that the development was not linked to any operational or governance concerns.

Atanu Chakraborty: Retired IAS officer whose abrupt exit from HDFC has raised eyebrowsIn his resignation letter, Atanu Chakraborty flagged concerns over the bank's internal functioning, stating that "certain happenings and practices within the bank" over the past two years were "not in congruence with my personal values and ethics".

HDFC Bank shares tumble 5%. Here's why the stock is down todayThe HDFC Bank stock emerged among the top losers on the benchmark indices, underperforming the broader market amid concerns around governance after Chakraborty stepped down citing issues related to "values and ethics".

Sensex crashes 2,500 points on oil shock, investors lose Rs 14 lakh croreMarkets slid sharply as rising oil prices, global tensions, and fresh worries around HDFC Bank spooked investors, triggering a broad sell-off and wiping out Rs 14 lakh crore in wealth.

Highlights: LPG supply intact, but concerns remain, Centre urges vigilanceLPG crisis Highlights: The government has flagged that the LPG supply situation continues to be under pressure and has urged consumers to use cooking gas carefully. At the same time, efforts are underway to expand piped natural gas networks to reduce reliance on LPG. In a major nationwide crackdown, officials said over 12,000 raids have been conducted and more than 15,000 cylinders seized to curb hoarding and black marketing under the Essential Commodities Act. Authorities are also encouraging commercial users in cities to shift to PNG, with simplified connections and incentives being offered. Online LPG bookings have climbed to around 94%, while commercial supplies are gradually resuming in several states with government support. Despite the arrival of fresh shipments like Shivalik and Nanda Devi, the effects of earlier disruptions are still being seen across sectors. Follow this live blog for the latest updates on LPG supply, government action and the situation across cities and industries in India.

Sensex, Nifty fall as crude oil surge rattles markets amid Middle East tensionsThe S&P BSE Sensex plummeted 1,446.01 points to 75,258.12, while the NSE Nifty50 lost 441.45 points to 23,336.35 as of 9:28 am.

entertainment.headline: Badshah gets interim protection from arrest amid Tateeree song row The Punjab and Haryana High Court has granted rapper Badshah interim protection from arrest in the controversy over his song Tateeree. This relief comes as the court stays coercive action amid allegations of obscene lyrics and portrayal of women, allowing the case to proceed under legal review.

This one stays with you: Sidharth Malhotra praises Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar 2 Sidharth Malhotra reviewed Ranveer Singh's espionage-thriller, Dhurandhar: The Revenge. He praised the film for its "unapologetic patriotism" and the performances of the lead cast.

BTS returns with ARIRANG, and ARMY is geared up to groove Ahead of ARIRANG, BTS and ARMY's bond stands as a testament to trust, intelligence, and mutual respect. From layered storytelling to fan-led decoding, let's look at how this fandom was never meant to be dismissed.

Nayyi Navelli: Yami Gautam announces her next on Dhurandhar 2 release day Yami Gautam announced her next film, Nayyi Navelli, on March 19, the day Dhurandhar: The Revenge hit theatres. The film promises a unique narrative set in Meerut, mixing drama and suspense with a fresh cinematic approach.

Dahaad 2, Panchayat 5 to Call Me Bae 2: Prime Video India releases its 2026 slate Prime Video unveiled its 2026 content slate. From Sonakshi Sinha's Dahaad 2, Ali Fazal's Raakh to Saba Azad's Storm, the line-up features a diverse mix of gripping dramas, dark comedies and thrilling crime stories.

Dhurandhar 2 actor Danish Iqbal reveals what went into playing Bade Saab Danish Iqbal opened up on playing Dawood Ibrahim, or Bade Saab, in Dhurandhar: The Revenge. He spoke about the secrecy around his role, the intense preparation, and the overwhelming response.

Saba Azad, Alaya F in Hrithik Roshan-produced thriller series Storm. First look The first look of Storm, produced by Hrithik Roshan, was released on March 19, 2026. Saba Azad and Alaya F play key roles in the upcoming show based on a housing scam.

Elvish Yadav calls Supreme Court snake venom case relief his 'Independence Day' Elvish Yadav reacted after the Supreme Court quashed criminal proceedings in the snake venom case. He called it his 'Independence Day', while talking about the years of scrutiny faced by him and his family.

Youth review: Ken Karunaas's school rom-com is teen drama done rightRelease Date:Thu Mar 19, 2026 Youth movie review: Director Ken Karunaas's school romance, starring himself, Anishma and Meenakshi, is a wholesome feel-good drama about teenagers. The film has the right doses of comedy, teen romance and emotions.

Dhurandhar 2 X reviews: Fans say film is grand, gritty, but not flawless Dhurandhar: The Revenge has sparked strong reactions online, with viewers praising its scale and Ranveer Singh's performance, while others feel it lacks the original's emotional depth. Its bold themes and visual spectacle have made it a major talking point despite mixed reviews.

NCW summons Nora Fatehi, Sanjay Dutt over Sarke Chunar song; hearing on March 24 The National Commission for Women (NCW) has summoned Nora Fatehi, Sanjay Dutt, and the makers of the song Sarke Chunar for a March 24 hearing following allegations of obscenity and sexually suggestive content.

Theatres erupt as PM Modi appears in Dhurandhar 2, internet can't keep calm Dhurandhar: The Revenge screenings saw loud cheers for the scenes where Prime Minister Narendra Modi appears. While theatres reacted positively, the moment also drew responses online, too.

Ustaad Bhagat Singh highlights: Pawan Kalyan film aims to be Ugadi gift Ustaad Bhagat Singh review and release live updates: Harish Shankar's Ustaad Bhagat Singh featuring Pawan Kalyan released on March 19. The film clashed with Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge.

Ustaad Bhagat Singh review: Another Pawan Kalyan film that takes audience for grantedRelease Date:Thu Mar 19, 2026 Ustaad Bhagat Singh movie review: Ustaad Bhagat Singh, starring Pawan Kalyan offers fans moments of charm but struggles with outdated storytelling.

Allu Arjun to Preity Zinta: Celebrities praise Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar 2 Dhurandhar 2 has impressed several celebrities, including Allu Arjun, Kartik Aaryan and Preity Zinta. The film, directed by Aditya Dhar and led by Ranveer Singh, released in the theatres on March 19.

Actors Divyanka Tripathi-Vivek Dahiya announce pregnancy: Plot twist after 10 years Actors Divyanka Tripathi and Vivek Dahiya announced pregnancy, shared that they kept the news private for six months, leaving fans pleasantly surprised.

Ram Gopal Varma says people want to marry Dhurandhar 2 in new remark Ram Gopal Varma has a striking new take on Dhurandhar 2, claiming audiences are deeply invested. His comments are adding to the film's growing buzz—here's why.

Aditya Dhar worked till 2:30 am before Dhurandhar 2 release, reveals Mukesh Chhabra Casting director Mukesh Chhabra praised Aditya Dhar's dedication to Dhurandhar and revealed he worked till 2:30 am before release. He called Dhar a genius and expressed confidence that the film would surpass expectations and break records.

Women National Commission summons Badshah over obscenity in Tateeree song The National Commission for Women has summoned singer Badshah and his team over obscenity in the song Tateeree, raising concerns about its lyrics and visuals under Indian laws.

general news.headline: European nations, Japan pledge efforts to ensure safe passage of vessels in HormuzIn a joint statement, five European nations and Japan said they would take coordinated steps to stabilise energy markets as tensions disrupt one of the world's most critical shipping routes.

Iran to face biggest strikes today: Trump aide says US to end Gulf war on its termsUS Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth said Iran will face the "largest strike package" again, asserting the Gulf war will end on US terms. He claimed major damage to Iran's military capabilities and vowed to continue operations until objectives are fully achieved.

Pak to get sucked into war? Saudi issues military threat as Iran strikes RiyadhThree weeks into the war in the Middle East, the Saudi Arabian capital, Riyadh, was targeted by Iranians using ballistic missiles. Riyadh has said that it might abandon the restraint and get involved militarily. With a defence pact in place, Pakistan could get sucked into the war, adding a nuclear dimension to the already volatile war.

Nashik 'Godman' drugged, raped woman for 3 years under religious guise, arrestedSelf-styled Nashik 'godman' Ashok Kharat has been arrested for allegedly raping a woman and exploiting others under the guise of religious practices.
With Pars on fire, humanity enters world of pain

What is the nuclear missile Pakistan is developing that US has warned aboutThe US has raised fresh concerns over Pakistan's long-range ballistic missile program. Pakistan's missile program has been historically focused on India. Now, the US fears that Islamabad is looking to expand its scope.

Video: Reporter escapes just in time as Israeli strike lands metres awayThe strike landed less than ten metres from the location where RT correspondent Steve Sweeney and his cameraman Ali Rada were filming in Lebanon.

Did US soldiers set fire to bedding, clog toilets of warship to skip war duty?Amid the war in Iran, the US aircraft carrier Gerald R Ford is retreating to a port in Greece for repairs. The ship was plagued by clogged toilets, and a fire that burnt through the vessel's laundry system and sleeping quarters. While the first has been blamed on the crew, the other could also be their handiwork of soldiers trying to skip war duty.

Dhurandhar 2 breaks all premiere records with Rs 52 crore, set for historic runRanveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge has begun its box office journey with record-breaking paid premiere collections exceeding Rs 52 crore.  The film is expected to dominate the box office with positive reviews from preview shows.

Decoding Congress Assam crisis: How BJP's strategic poaching deepens electoral woesBJP's first list of candidates for the upcoming Assam Assembly elections also featured the names of two former Congress turncoats, Pradyut Bordoloi and Bhupen Borah.

Like Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli, Sanju Samson cannot be replaced: Riyan ParagIPL: Rajasthan Royals captain Riyan Parag has said that like Virat Kohli or Rohit Sharma, Sanju Samson, is irreplaceable as a player. Parag is set to take over as the full time captain of the side this year.

Live: Iran warns of 'zero restraint' if Israel targets infrastructure againUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The United States was informed in advance of Israel's strike on Iran's South Pars gas field but did not take part, as global oil prices surged on fears of retaliation. Hours later, a missile hit Qatar's key gas facility, causing significant damage and prompting Doha to expel some Iranian embassy officials. Israel also killed Iran's intelligence minister, further destabilising its leadership, as Tehran warned of retaliation. Meanwhile, an Iranian missile struck a building in the occupied West Bank, killing three people and injuring at least 13 — the first fatalities there since the Iran war began. Stay tuned to IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

Watch: Virat Kohli effortlessly takes down spinners in RCB netsIPL: RCB talisman Virat Kohli has started training at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium. Ahead of the IPL opener, Kohli was seen in some solid touch in the RCB nets.

Can Iran shut down global internet by cutting Hormuz undersea cables?The world is facing an energy crisis as Iran blocked supplies passing through the Strait of Hormuz after strikes by the US and Israel. Vital subsea cables that carry global internet traffic also pass through Hormuz. Any damage to them could trigger internet outages and hit financial systems across countries, including in India.

Father, 2 children among 4 shot at in Delhi home; gang war suspectedA suspected gang war incident was reported in Delhi's Bawana area, where armed assailants barged into a house and opened fire. Four people sustained bullet injuries in the attack, including a young boy who later succumbed.

'Trimester zero' and the new anxiety around getting pregnant 'right'Probably even seven or eight years ago, planning a baby didn't involve going on a full-fledged diet or detox to prepare the body for pregnancy. Now, it's a thing, a welcome change, and that phase is called the 'zero trimester'.

California heat dome shatters 100-year records. Here's howA ferocious heat dome has pushed temperatures past 100 degrees Fahrenheit across California, Nevada and Arizona, smashing records that stood for over a century. Meteorologists warn peak heat is still ahead, with relief not expected until early next week.

3 IIT students killed as car loses control, overturns on Mumbai-Pune ExpresswayThree IIT students were killed and one critically injured after a speeding car lost control and overturned multiple times on the Mumbai-Pune Expressway.

Ranveer Singh credits Rakesh Bedi for Rs 500 crore of Dhurandhar 2's Rs 1000 croreRakesh Bedi receives acclaim for his role as Jamali in Dhurandhar 2, with cast and crew highlighting the camaraderie on set and the sequel anticipated to surpass the first film's box office success.

Ranveer Singh leads a film that knows exactly the impact it wants to leave behind.

Is winter back? Delhi set for a cold Friday as heavy rain rolls inDelhi is set to witness one of its coldest days in March on Friday, with maximum temperatures plunging to near-winter levels amid heavy rain and thunderstorms. The IMD has warned of rare cold-day conditions across North India, with hailstorms expected in several states.

The lost Aravalli city: Tracing Delhi's forgotten origins before Sultanate and Mughal eraDelhi's story did not begin with the Sultanate or the Mughals. Along the Aravalli ridge, the Tomars and Chauhans once shaped the city's earliest settlements and Surajkund remains one of the few places where that forgotten chapter of Delhi's past still endures...

sports.headline: Watch: Virat Kohli effortlessly takes down spinners in RCB nets IPL: RCB talisman Virat Kohli has started training at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium. Ahead of the IPL opener, Kohli was seen in some solid touch in the RCB nets.

CSK scamper for replacements as star pacer ruled out due to hamstring injury IPL: Chennai Super Kings are hunting for a replacement for their star fast bowler Nathan Ellis. CSK CEO has confirmed that Ellis, has been ruled out of the season due to a hamstring injury.

Iran-Israel war's odd effect on cricket: England are running out of balls England's domestic cricket season faces an odd disruption due to the Iran-Israel conflict. It seems that the war has hit the supply of Dukes balls, leaving counties with limited stock ahead of their new season.

Like Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli, Sanju Samson cannot be replaced: Riyan Parag IPL: Rajasthan Royals captain Riyan Parag has said that like Virat Kohli or Rohit Sharma, Sanju Samson, is irreplaceable as a player. Parag is set to take over as the full time captain of the side this year.

PBKS will rectify mistakes from the IPL 2025 final: Shashank Singh IPL 2026: Punjab Kings all-rounder Shashank Singh emphasised that the side will work on their drawbacks shown in the 2025 final, and go the full distance in the upcoming season. PBKS lost the IPL 2025 final to RCB and missed out on a maiden title.

Never seen PCB this weak: Mohsin Naqvi and Co. slammed by Shehzad Ahmed Shehzad has slammed the Pakistan cricket board and Mohsin Naqvi for enabling star culture within the team and allowing the players to take control of the national side. Pakistan suffered a Super 8 exit from T20 World Cup and were beaten by Bangladesh in the ODI series recently.

Watch: CSK's new star Sanju Samson arrives in Chennai ahead of IPL 2026 IPL 2026: Sanju Samson has arrived in Chennai, and the buzz is already building. The new CSK recruit enters IPL 2026 on the back of a dream T20 World Cup, with expectations sky-high.

Gautam Gambhir moves Delhi HC against deepfakes, AI face-swapping misuse Gautam Gambhir has moved the Delhi High Court against AI deepfakes and impersonation. The India head coach has sought legal protection over the misuse of his identity online.

Karthik, Pallikal blessed with 3rd child, Kohli, sporting world react Former India cricketer Dinesh Karthik and squash star Dipika Pallikal announced the birth of their third child on Thursday, March 19. Virat Kohli, Sania Mirza and others led the way with wishes for the couple on social media.

Knight Riders get a new home, become first franchise with own stadium in California IPL 2026: The Knight Riders' global expansion has taken a concrete step in the USA. LA Knight Riders will now operate from a fixed base in California, becoming the first IPL-owned franchise to have a dedicated home ground.

Ishan Kishan as SRH captain is a beautiful story: Ex-India batter back IPL 2026 call IPL 2026: Ishan Kishan's rise continues to gather pace after his T20 World Cup heroics. Aakash Chopra has backed Sunrisers Hyderabad's decision to name him stand-in captain, calling it a fitting reward for his comeback journey.

Buttler hopes to keep England dreams alive after T20 World Cup 2026 flop showJos Buttler is hoping that he can continue playing for England, despite his poor showing during the T20 World Cup 2026. Buttler admitted that the final decision will be left in the hands of the selectors.

African football body chief defends decision to strip Senegal's AFCON titleThe African Cup of Nations 2026 final controversy has taken a new turn after Senegal were stripped of their title. CAF president Patrice Motsepe has denied corruption claims and defended the organisation's decision.

Arjun Tendulkar leaves Rishabh Pant impressed with Sachin-style technique in LSG campIPL 2026: Arjun Tendulkar has already made heads turn at his new IPL camp. The young all-rounder left LSG captain Rishabh Pant impressed with his bat weight and a technique that clearly reflects his father Sachin Tendulkar's influence.

Galatasaray's Noa Lang suffers freak finger injury vs LiverpoolGalatasaray endured a night to forget in the Champions League as injuries piled up during their heavy loss to Liverpool. Noa Lang suffered a freak finger injury while Victor Osimhen picked up a painful arm issue.

Hey Ancelotti, what about me? Neymar's reaction to Brazil squad snub goes viralNeymar's reaction to being snubbed from Brazil's squad for the friendlies against France and Croatia has gone viral as he jokingly asked Carlo Ancelotti why he wasn't in the final list.

Yuzvendra Chahal's shock reveal before IPL 2026: Stopped drinking alcoholYuzvendra Chahal has revealed that he has decided to keep himself sober and away from alcohol in a bid to get ready for the IPL 2026 season.

Liverpool dismantle Galatasaray, Barcelona hit 7 past NewcastleLiverpool overturned a first-leg deficit to thrash Galatasaray at home to make it to the quarterfinals of the UEFA Champions League. Barcelona dismantled Newcastle and beat them 7-2 to book their spot in the final 8.

IPL 2026: KKR target ex-CSK pace duo as Harshit Rana replacement optionsAmid the injury concern to Harshit Rana, KKR are targeting ex-CSK pace duo Simarjeet Singh and KM Asif and Sandeep Warrier as potential replacements for the star pacer ahead of the IPL 2026 season.

Iran withdraws FIFA World Cup boycott threat, but refuses to play matches in USAIran have said that they will not boycott the upcoming FIFA World Cup 2026 but remained firm on their stance that they will not play any of the matches in the USA amid the ongoing West Asia conflict.

Ishan Kishan keeps rewriting his story, from India outcast to SRH captainIPL 2026: Ishan Kishan faces the challenging task of leading Sunrisers Hyderabad in Pat Cummins' absence, but given the kind of 2026 he has enjoyed so far, all eyes will be on how the Jharkhand batter rises to the occasion in the IPL.

Virat Kohli has a squad of fighters: AB de Villiers backs RCB to defend IPL crownIPL 2026: Former South African batter AB de Villiers backed RCB to win consecutive titles, praising their strong, balanced squad and saying the team is no longer solely reliant on Virat Kohli.

India vs Pakistan on August 19 as FIH unveils Hockey World Cup 2026 scheduleIndia and Pakistan will face off in a highly anticipated pool-stage clash on August 19 at the FIH Hockey World Cup 2026, held across Belgium and the Netherlands from August 15 to 30.

Can SRH survive IPL 2026 without captain Pat Cummins?IPL: Sunrisers Hyderabad captain Pat Cummins has been ruled out of the first few matches of the IPL. How big is this blow? Can SRH survive games without their mercurial captain?

PCB source revealsBabar Azam and Fakhar Zaman may not have been fully fit when they were selected for Pakistan's T20 World Cup squad, with the PCB expected to investigate after concerns were raised by senior selector Aaqib Javed and the board's medical panel.

Iran women's football team return home days after seeking asylum in AustraliaThe Iranian women's football team safely returned home from Australia, days after claiming asylum on humanitarian grounds. 5 players withdrew their claim despite earlier fears of persecution amid rising geopolitical tensions.

Spin-friendly pitch can be KKR's key to success in IPL: Faf du PlessisIPL: Kolkata Knight Riders' key to success in the Indian Premier League will depend on the kind of pitches that they make, believes Faf du Plessis. KKR endured a difficult season at home last time.

Ishan Kishan fit enough as T20 captain? Here's what numbers say before IPL 2026IPL 2026: Ishan Kishan has been named Sunrisers Hyderabad's stand-in captain in the absence of Pat Cummins. The left-hander heads into the IPL on the back of a sensational T20 World Cup 2026 campaign.

India drawn with China in Thomas and Uber Cups 2026India have been handed tough draws in both the Thomas Cup and Uber Cup 2026 after being grouped with defending champions China. The BWF announced the draws for the competition, which is set to held  between April 24 and May 3.

FC Barcelona will be Hansi Flick's last coaching jobHansi Flick says Barcelona will be his last club as a coach, ahead of a crucial Champions League second leg against Newcastle at Camp Nou on Wednesday.

SRH name Ishan Kishan as stand-in captain in absence of Pat CumminsIPL 2026: Wicketkeeper batter Ishan Kishan has been named the captain of SunRisers Hyderabad in the absence of Pat Cummins, who is set to miss the first few IPL games for the franchise. Kishan's promotion comes in the backdrop of his sensational outing in the T20 World Cup 2026.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
